subject: Cloud Computing
title: Misconfiguration: The Top Threat
description: A short lesson to help you apply Misconfiguration: The Top Threat.

hook.text: Capital One lost 100 million customer records in 2019. The cause? A misconfigured firewall rule.

buildup.text: Misconfiguration is the number one cloud security threat. Open storage buckets, weak IAM, exposed ports.

discovery.text: Cloud Security Posture Management tools scan for misconfigurations automatically and flag risks.

twist.text: Teams fix misconfigurations, then someone deploys a new resource and reintroduces the same mistake.

climax.text: Prevention beats detection. Enforce security policies in code so misconfigurations can't be deployed.

punchline.text: The biggest cloud threat isn't hackers. It's misconfiguration.

quiz.question: What caused the Capital One data breach in 2019?
options: A misconfigured firewall rule that exposed customer data
A physical break-in at an AWS data center
An employee intentionally leaking data to competitors
